topic.what: DP synthetic data generators MST and AIM privacy auditing via GDP framework
topic.how: MIA-based distinguishing game with full FPR-FNR tradeoff and Bayesian lower-bound estimation for mu
topic.why_matters: First tight audit of MST/AIM in strong-privacy regime (epsilon=1); validates implementation correctness and reveals small theory-practice gap

characteristics.what_it_measures: Empirical privacy guarantee via GDP parameter
characteristics.evidence: Bayesian 90% posterior lower bound from XGBoost MIA on 4K test samples
characteristics.context: Strong-privacy regime: epsilon=1, delta=1e-2; 10K models trained; synthetic size 50

characteristics.what_it_measures: Implied theoretical privacy guarantee via zCDP accounting
characteristics.vs_baseline: Derived from rho-zCDP where rho maps to (epsilon=1, delta=1e-2)
characteristics.evidence: Internal conversion via Canonne et al. (epsilon,delta)->rho->mu path
characteristics.context: MST/AIM internally convert (epsilon,delta) to rho-zCDP then to mu-GDP

characteristics.what_it_measures: Gap between empirical and theoretical mu
characteristics.context: Significantly narrower than prior audits reporting zero/null estimates

characteristics.what_it_measures: MIA false positive rate at optimal threshold
characteristics.vs_baseline: Varied across thresholds; selected tau* maximizing advantage
characteristics.evidence: XGBoost classifier on 4K test samples at tau*=0.48

characteristics.context: Stable across thresholds; optimal at tau*=0.48

characteristics.what_it_measures: Previous methods fail to detect leakage
characteristics.vs_baseline: Prior work reports epsilonemp=0 in strong-privacy regime
characteristics.evidence: Annamalai et al. 2024, dpmm library audits
characteristics.context: Prior methods produce loose/null results due to unstable threshold selection

novelty: First tight audit of MST/AIM in strong-privacy regime (epsilon=1)
GDP-centric auditing with full FPR-FNR tradeoff curve
Bayesian lower-bound mu estimation for privacy leakage
Reveals theory-practice gap is narrow (~4%) vs prior null results
open_problems: Auditing under full configuration with higher-order marginals
Validating frequentist coverage of Bayesian bounds
Extending to other DP generative models beyond MST/AIM
